channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Nnadozie anticipates WAFCON surprises
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Super Falcons goalkeeper Chiamaka Nnadozie warns of no easy opponents at WAFCON 2026, urging respect for all teams as Nigeria aims for the title.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nnadozie-anticipates-wafcon-surprises/

item.title: 13 suspected kidnappers, others face trial in Osun
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
13 suspected kidnappers, cultists, and ritualists are set to face trial in Osun State after police operations led to arrests and ransom recovery. Read more
Read More: https://punchng.com/13-suspected-kidnappers-others-face-trial-in-osun/

item.title: Spain has shut lots of mouths – Cubarsi
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Spain’s Pau Cubarsí has praised his side’s defensive display in their dominant World Cup semi-final win against France. The post Spain has shut lots of mouths – Cubarsi appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Mbappe admits France got tactics wrong against Spain
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Kylian Mbappe rued the end of France’s World Cup dream on Tuesday, blaming tactical and technical blunders for his side’s 2-0 semi-final defeat to France. The post Mbappe admits France got tactics wrong against Spain appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Extended half-time musical show for final
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The half-time break during Sunday’s FIFA World Cup final is expected to be longer than usual as organisers plan a Super Bowl-style musical performance featuring several international stars. The post Extended half-time musical show for final appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: After WC loss, Olise wants move to Real Madrid
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: With France now eliminated from the 2026 FIFA World Cup, focus will once again turn to Michael Olise’s future at club level. As per a report from Foot Mercato, the France international wants to complete a move to Real Madrid this summer. The post After WC loss, Olise wants move to Real Madrid appear

item.title: Rodri makes FIFA complaint about Lamine Yamal treatment
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Rodri has criticised FIFA for the standard of officiating at the World Cup after Spain booked their place in the final on Tuesday night. Spain recorded an impressive 2-0 victory over pre-tournament favourites France to set up a potentially mouth-watering showdown against either England or Argentina

item.title: Aina, Awoniyi report for pre-season training
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Super Eagles stars were among the group that returned to the Nigel Doughty Academy on Monday, July 13, to begin preparations for the upcoming Premier League campaign, Forest’s fifth consecutive season in the top flight. The post Aina, Awoniyi report for pre-season training appeared first on The

item.title: 13 rescued in foiled Zamfara terrorists’ operations
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian troops successfully rescued 13 kidnap victims in Zamfara during operations that disrupted planned terrorist activities. Read more about the Zamfar
Read More: https://punchng.com/13-rescued-in-foiled-zamfara-terrorists-operations/

item.title: WAFU B U20 Championship: Flying Eagles to play Ghana, Togo, Burkina Faso
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigeria’s U20 boys, Flying Eagles, have been drawn to play Ghana’s Black Satellites, Togo’s Junior Sparrow Hawks and Burkina Faso’s Young Stallions in the group phase of this year’s West Africa Football Union (WAFU) B U20 Championship to be hosted by Cote d’Ivoire. The post WAFU B U20 Championship:

item.title: Six killed as gas truck explodes at Kogi Army checkpoint
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A gas truck explosion at an Army checkpoint on the Abuja-Lokoja highway in Kogi State has killed six people. Read more about the tragic Kogi gas explosion.
Read More: https://punchng.com/six-killed-as-gas-truck-explodes-at-kogi-army-checkpoint/

item.title: 14th WAFCON: I believe in Super Falcons – Ihezuo
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former junior international striker Chinwendu Ihezuo has admitted that the 14th edition of the Women Africa Cup of Nations finals taking place in Morocco 26th July – 16th August will be much tougher than the last competition held in the same North African Kingdom a year ago. However, she believes th

item.title: Joshua: Fury can wait until I beat Prenga
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Anthony Joshua says Tyson Fury will have to wait before any face-to-face moment between the two, insisting Fury can only step into the ring once Joshua has beaten Kristian Prenga on July 25 in Jeddah. The post Joshua: Fury can wait until I beat Prenga appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Igali tips Team Nigeria for Glasgow glory, hails NSC
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former Olympic and world wrestling champion Daniel Igali has thrown his weight behind Team Nigeria’s medal ambitions at the 2026 Commonwealth Games, saying the improved welfare and meticulous preparations put in place by the National Sports Commission (NSC) have given the country’s athletes the plat

item.title: Trump says Iran released 'wrongfully detained' American citizen
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US President Donald Trump said Iran had released an American citizen detained since December 2024 and allowed her to leave the country, calling it a "gesture of goodwill" despite renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: Five-year-old rescued as police arrest two suspected kidnappers
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Oyo State Police rescued a five-year-old kidnap victim and arrested two suspects who demanded N10m ransom. Read more about the child kidnapping case.
Read More: https://punchng.com/five-year-old-rescued-as-police-arrest-two-suspected-kidnappers/

item.title: 75-year-old Rivers man bags 10 years jail for defiling minor
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A 75-year-old Rivers man, Promise Chukwumatti, has been sentenced to 10 years in prison for defiling an 11-year-old minor. The court also ordered N1m compe
Read More: https://punchng.com/75-year-old-rivers-man-bags-10-years-jail-for-defiling-minor/

item.title: Iran launches strikes on Gulf, even as FM visits Qatar
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Iranian FM Araghchi visits Qatar to pay respects following the death of the Father Emir, Sheikh Hamad bin al Thani

item.title: Zobo, promising weapon against drug-resistant UTI bacteria, experts say
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Researchers at the Federal University Dutsin-Ma (FUDMA), Katsina State, have identified Hibiscus sabdariffa, popularly known as Zobo, as a promising natural antimicrobial agent against multidrug-resistant Escherichia coli (E. coli), the leading cause of urinary tract infections (UTIs). In a new stud

item.title: Kyiv under fire from Russian missiles after EU-Ukraine sign drone deal
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Mayor Vitali Klitschko says emergency services are battling blazes across the Ukrainian capital.

item.title: France’s Morocco reset: What French PM Lecornu’s visit to Rabat signals
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The trip highlights how Paris and Rabat are turning a diplomatic rapprochement into a broader strategic partnership.

item.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran as Trump warns Tehran it 'better behave'
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he is yet to decide whether or not he will "finish off" Iran.

item.title: FG tackling Nigeria’s learning crisis — Alausa
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Federal Government has intensified efforts to address Nigeria’s learning crisis with the commencement of a nationwide National Learning Assessment aimed at measuring pupils’ literacy, numeracy and cognitive skills to drive evidence-based reforms in the education sector. The post FG tackling Nige

item.title: Wildlife expert seeks snake protection laws to curb Lassa fever
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A wildlife expert urges the Nigerian government to enact snake protection laws to control rodent populations and reduce Lassa fever spread.
Read More: https://punchng.com/wildlife-expert-seeks-snake-protection-laws-to-curb-lassa-fever/

item.title: NBA presidency: Senior advocates endorse Badejo-Okusanya, seek transformative leadership
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Senior Advocates of Nigeria endorse Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya for NBA Presidency, citing her vision for transformative leadership and reform.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nba-presidency-senior-advocates-endorse-badejo-okusanya-seek-transformative-leadership/

item.title: Iranian doctor says US strike damaged children’s cancer hospital
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Iranians are inspecting the damage from days of US strikes on key cities in the country’s south and west.

item.title: Why England's loss to Argentina felt like most painful in 60 years of hurt
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England's years of hurt will now stretch beyond 60, but the late collapse to a World Cup semi-final defeat by Argentina may be the most painful wound of all.

item.title: Nigeria’s domestic Jet A1 pricing defies ‘global platts’ benchmark
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A fall in global crude oil prices would ordinarily be expected to ease the cost of aviation fuel and, by extension, reduce airline ticket prices. The post Nigeria’s domestic Jet A1 pricing defies ‘global platts’ benchmark appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Activist demands probe of Umahi physiotherapist’s death
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A human rights activist is demanding a comprehensive probe into the mysterious death of Mary Habila, Dave Umahi’s physiotherapist, at his Uburu residence.
Read More: https://punchng.com/activist-demands-probe-of-umahi-physiotherapists-death/

item.title: ‘Foreign fleets maintain dominance in Nigeria’s maritime support industry’
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Foreign fleets still dominate Nigeria’s maritime support industry, despite being Africa’s largest oil and gas producer. Discover how this impacts the econo
Read More: https://punchng.com/foreign-fleets-maintain-dominance-in-nigerias-maritime-support-industry/

item.title: Celebrations erupt in Argentina’s capital after World Cup semifinal win
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Jubilant fans leapt with joy in Buenos Aires after Argentina defeated England 2-1 in Wednesday’s World Cup semifinal.

item.title: Universities are for legacy, not profit-making, Ooni declares
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Ooni of Ife, Oba Adeyeye Enitan Ogunwusi, and the Owa Obokun of Ijesaland, Oba Clement Adesuyi Haastrup, have extolled the virtues of the late vice chancellor of the University of Lagos (UNILAG), Professor Oyewusi Ibidapo-Obe, praising his immense contributions to university education and societ

item.title: India Raises Export Levies on Diesel and Jet Fuel
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: India increased taxes on exports of ‌diesel and aviation turbine fuel as the collapse of the US-Iran interim peace deal and renewed hostilities have choked oil flows again tightening global fuel markets.

item.title: Middle East live: US launches fresh strikes on Iran as Hormuz remains paralyzed
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The United States launched a fresh wave of strikes against Iran on Wednesday, targeting military capabilities linked to threats against shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. President Donald Trump warned he could widen the campaign unless Tehran returned to negotiations. Follow our liveblog for the late

item.title: NCAT assures ATSSSAN of better workers’ welfare
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Nigerian College of Aviation Technology (NCAT), Zaria, has announced a new welfare measure that allows members of staff to proceed on study leave with pay, as part of efforts to improve employee development and motivation. The post NCAT assures ATSSSAN of better workers’ welfare appeared first o

item.title: Modi Pushes to Cut Import Reliance to Shield India From Shocks
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is taking steps to reduce key imports into the economy in order to protect supply chains and ease pressure on the currency as geopolitical risks escalate.

item.title: AI is so big, it’s now impossible for investors to avoid
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Exposure to the artificial-intelligence boom has become virtually impossible for investors to avoid. The theme hasn’t just cornered the stock market. It has systematically swallowed up corporate credit and venture capital, too.

item.title: 'Passive' and 'crumbled' - did Tuchel's defensive tactics cost England?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England went 1-0 up against Argentina before slumping to a heart-breaking semi-final defeat. But did boss Thomas Tuchel's "negative" tactics cost the Three Lions a place in the World Cup final?

item.title: UNILAG don advocates national policy shift on workplace wellness
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A professor of Political Science and Dean, Faculty of Social Sciences, University of Lagos (UNILAG), Adelaja Odukoya, has warned that Nigeria cannot achieve enduring economic growth by exhausting its workforce. The post UNILAG don advocates national policy shift on workplace wellness appeared first

item.title: Wildfire smoke engulfs Toronto, Canada and turns its skies orange
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Wildfire smoke turned skies orange over Toronto and the rest of Ontario.

item.title: Japan PE Fund Seeks $1.9 Billion Economic-Security Investments
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Advantage Partners LLC plans to invest in big domestic companies posing economic security risks for Japan that might benefit from government backing and boost profits in the long term.

item.title: Anambra nonagenarian leads protest over communal land dispute
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Vincent Ujumadu A 96-year-old man, Elder Stephen Okoye, on Wednesday led hundreds of residents of Umuenu Quarters in Umuawulu, Awka South Local Government Area of Anambra State, in a protest over what they alleged was an attempt by the state’s Deputy Governor, Dr. Onyekachukwu Ibezim, to in

item.title: Defeat will haunt England for a long time, says Burn
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England defender Dan Burn says the World Cup semi-final defeat by Argentina will haunt him.

item.title: Nigeria, Morocco agree deal on agricultural intelligence food security
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: —-To establish geospatial crop monitoring, food security planning By Johnbosco Agbakwuru ABUJA— THE Presidency on Wednesday sent a high-level delegation to Morocco to finalise a partnership for developing Nigeria’s first National Agro-Productivity System. The delegation is headed by Sena

item.title: Korea Plans Measures on Leveraged ETFs Driving Wild Volatility
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: South Korea will soon announce measures regarding the leveraged exchange-traded funds tied to shares of Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. that have fueled a surge in market volatility.

item.title: Arsenal Forward, Trossard, Joins Besiktas for £15.3m
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Belgium forward Leandro Trossard has joined Turkish side Besiktas from Arsenalon a three-year contract. The 31-year-old has been signed for 18m euros (£15.3m) – paid in six equal instalments over three

item.title: Bournemouth in Talks for Benfica Defender
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Bournemouthare in talks with Benfica to sign Portugal defender Antonio Silva as a replacement for Marcos Senesi. The Cherries have targeted the 22-year-old following Senesi’s decision to leaveon a free transfer

item.title: Sunderland Sign Belgium World Cup Defender Meunier
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Sunderlandhave signed Belgium defender Thomas Meunier on a two-year deal following his departure from French side Lille. The 34-year-old has made more than 550 senior club appearances and previously had

item.title: Under Eyesan, NUPRC Resets Nigeria’s Upstream Oil Industry

item.description: Six months after taking over the leadership of the Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC), Mrs. Oritsemeyiwa Eyesan is beginning to put her stamp on an institution central to Nigeria’s

item.title: United Nigeria Airlines empowers visually impaired singers at talent show
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Domestic carrier, United Nigeria Airlines, at the weekend, supported young talents who took part in a singing competition organised for people living with disabilities. The post United Nigeria Airlines empowers visually impaired singers at talent show appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Tuchel retains FA backing despite England defeat
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Thomas Tuchel retains the backing of the Football Association despite England failing to reach the World Cup final.

item.title: United Airlines gets hit by a $6 billion added-fuel-cost headwind
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Investors zero in on United’s potentially fatter jet-fuel bill — the airline said it expects nearly $6 billion in additional fuel expenses for the year.

item.title: The trucking market is tighter than it’s been in years — but investors are upbeat on J.B. Hunt
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Shares of the trucking giant jump 8% after hours, as fuel charges and demand for its railroad-connection business lift results

item.title: Why Greylock capped its new fund at $1.5B when it says it could have raised more
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: By keeping the number of investments to about 25 per fund, Greylock aims to remain what it calls "the most important partner" to its founders.

item.title: NUC reaffirms commitment to accelerate innovation enterpreneurship in Nigerian varsities
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The National Universities Commission (NUC) has reaffirmed its commitment to accelerating the development of innovation and entrepreneurship ecosystems across Nigerian universities through strategic international partnerships. The post NUC reaffirms commitment to accelerate innovation enterpreneurshi

item.title: NPC Launches Nationwide Digital Birth, Death Registration System
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The National Population Commission (NPC) has launched a nationwide digital registration system for births and deaths aimed at modernising civil registration and improving Nigeria's demographic data management. The post NPC Launches Nationwide Digital Birth, Death Registration System appeared first o

item.title: ‘Epic squared’: Scaloni lauds Argentina’s back-to-back World Cup comebacks
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: 'There was blood in the water, and we went for it,' Argentina coach said of the relentless late attacks against England.

item.title: Prince William tells England to 'hold heads high'
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: The Prince of Wales says England has "inspired us all" as he offers his commiserations over the loss to Argentina.

item.title: IEA Boss Warns Global Economy in Peril If Hormuz Crisis Persists
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The global economy faces a renewed challenge if the conflict that’s choked the Strait of Hormuz isn’t resolved in a matter of weeks, said International Energy Agency Executive Director Fatih Birol.

item.title: Oriire saga: Who is afraid of an international probe?
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: IT is hard to think of a fair-minded Nigerian who would oppose Governor Seyi Makinde’s call for a UN-backed investigation into the Oriire abduction The post Oriire saga: Who is afraid of an international probe? appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Tuchel vows to remain as England head coach after Argentina defeat
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Thomas Tuchel says he will stay on to lead England into Euro 2028 despite their World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina.

item.title: BHP Iron Ore Workers Stage First Port Hedland Strike Since 2000
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Workers at BHP Group’s Port Hedland operations in Western Australia will stage the first strike at the company’s Pilbara iron ore hub since 2000, after last-ditch talks failed to avert industrial action.

item.title: Big Brother Naija Season 11 Premieres 26 July With a Record ₦160 Million Grand Prize
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: Big Brother Naija returns on Sunday 26 July 2026 for its eleventh season, themed "Everything Is For The Taking." Hosted by Ebuka Obi-Uchendu, the ten-week show features a historic ₦160 million grand prize. The post Big Brother Naija Season 11 Premieres 26 July With a Record ₦160 Million Grand Prize

item.title: How global heating supercharged floods in West Africa, displacing thousands
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: Adaptation to frightening new normal and reducing emissions further and faster is critical, scientists warnDozens of people drowned, hundreds had to be rescued and thousands were displaced when floods struck the coasts of west Africa last month.Now scientists have concluded that the rains that cause

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Iranian media report explosions on Qeshm Island, Bandar Abbas and Chabahar, as Bahrain and Kuwait sound air raid sirens.

item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Skullcandy announced a new version of its Crusher wireless headphones today featuring a few of Bose's audio technologies including its QuietControl ANC and head-tracking spatial audio. The Crusher headphone line differentiates itself from the competition through the use of both full-range and dedica

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: England manager Tuchel swapped Reece James for Dan Burn, and Declan Rice for defender Nico O’Reilly in the 82nd minute.

item.title: IMF, President Tinubu and the transparency challenge
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Moshood Oshunfurewa If we are to reincarnate the late Chief Gani Oyesola Fawehinmi (1938–2009), a legendary Nigerian human rights lawyer, publisher, author, social critic, and Senior Advocate of the Masses (SAM), on the current budget imbroglio, 2% of GDP, and Gbaja/Adeyemi scandal, I might not m

item.title: NFF: Need for a new beginning
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Nigeria’s absence from the 2026 World Cup is not fate. It is the bill finally arriving for years of reckless housekeeping at the Nigeria Football Federation (NFF), and those responsible must be made to pay for it. Our football history shows that Nigeria rarely loses to better teams. It loses to itse

item.title: Liquidity in Malaysia May Tighten Further as Rate-Hike Bets Grow
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Funding conditions in Malaysia’s money market will likely tighten further later this year, with some analysts expecting stronger economic growth to prompt the country’s central bank to raise interest rates.

item.title: Goodluck Jonathan: Nigeria’s most cowardly politician! By Olu Fasan
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Twice in recent presidential election cycles, Goodluck Jonathan, Nigeria’s former one-term president, allowed speculations to spread like wildfire that he would seek the presidency again. But twice, without as much as a whimper, the speculations fizzled out into thin air like a puff of wind. Recentl

item.title: KWASU research teams secure $43,913 TWAS-UNESCO, N83m NRF grants
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Four research teams from Kwara State University (KWASU) have secured competitive research grants from two renowned funding organisations, reaffirming the university’s growing reputation for research excellence. The post KWASU research teams secure $43,913 TWAS-UNESCO, N83m NRF grants appeared first

item.title: Oyo abduction and Makinde’s call for UN probe, by Ikechukwu Amaechi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: There is no well-meaning Nigerian who is not relieved that the Oyo State kidnap saga ended happily despite the eight fatalities and the 56 agonising days it took to embrace the light at the end of what was, to all intents and purposes, a very dark chapter in Nigeria’s beleaguered history. The mass a

item.title: The high cost of condescension: Why workplace feedback needs dignity upgrade, by Ruth Oji
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Imagine this scenario: a senior manager publicly interrogates a junior employee about a minor reporting error. “Did you even think before submitting this?” he asked, voice dripping with contempt, as colleagues shifted uncomfortably in their seats. “What exactly goes through your mind when you do suc

item.description: BBC Sport looks at Lionel Messi's masterclass on the right wing to knock England out of the World Cup.

item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Micah Richards pays tribute to his father Lincoln, who died shortly before the BBC pundit went on air for England's World Cup semi-final defeat by Argentina.

item.title: Argentina players brandish political Falklands flag after England match
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The banner, held up by Lisandro Martinez and Giovani Lo Celso, goes against FIFA’s Stadium Code of Conduct.

item.title: Kane: Holding on was not enough against Argentina
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: England captain Harry Kane admitted trying to hold on to a 1-0 lead backfired as their World Cup hopes were ended by Argentina's late show in a 2-1 semi-final defeat.

item.title: NACCIMA, Chinese Partners Sign MoU To Drive FDI, Boost Nigeria-China Trade
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigeria Association of Chambers of Commerce, Industry, Mines and Agriculture (NACCIMA) has signed a tripartite Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with China’s Linyi Wholesalers Federation and TTC Hydra Limited to deepen bilateral trade and facilitate fresh Chinese investments into Nigeria.

item.title: The transition that must not fail…
item.dc:creator: Tribune Opinion
item.description: IN Nigeria’s legal profession, few distinctions command as much respect. Recently I was in the Oke-Mosan area of Abeokuta, the Ogun State capital, standing in front of the Kings’ Court Estate, watching workers lay the final blocks. The post The transition that must not fail… appeared first on

item.title: Uzodimma Invites Tinubu To Commission Legacy Projects In Imo
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Imo State Governor, Senator Hope Uzodimma, on Tuesday met with President Bola Ahmed Tinubu at the Presidential Villa, Abuja, where the duo held extensive discussions on issues of national development, security, economic growth and ongoing infrastructure development in Imo State The post Uzodimma Inv

item.title: NITDA, FUET Deepen Collaboration On Digital Infrastructure, Skills Development
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Information Technology Development Agency (NITDA) has reaffirmed its commitment to strengthening Nigeria’s higher education sector through strategic investments in digital infrastructure, innovation and digital skills development. This is part of the moves to establish a collaborative p

item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://playonemoreletter.com/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48928402 Points: 41 # Comments: 29

item.title: Kan Ilekun” Rite Sets Stage For Promotion Of Ibadan Chiefs By Olubadan
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Ajia Olubadan of Ibadan Land, Oloye Akin Alabi, has announced the commencement of the promotion of traditional chiefs in the Olubadan line The post Kan Ilekun” Rite Sets Stage For Promotion Of Ibadan Chiefs By Olubadan appeared first on Trending News.

item.title: Ex-Speaker Etteh, Abbas’ wife, others press lawmakers to pass women’s representation bill
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The Constitution Alteration Bill proposes the creation of additional seats exclusively for women in the National Assembly and state Houses of Assembly as a temporary affirmative action measure. The post Ex-Speaker Etteh, Abbas’ wife, others press lawmakers to pass women’s representation bill a

item.title: Nul Characters in Strings in SQLite
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: New monkey species with orange lips found 'hiding' in DRC forest
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Scientists describe "amazing feeling" to look into the face of an animal that so few people knew existed.
